title: The Official Mister RogersтАЩ Neighborhood YouTube Channel Goes Live: Watch Complete Episodes, Including the Very First
description:
in Television |   	June 8th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

A great many, and per┬нhaps the major┬нi┬нty of Amer┬нi┬нcans now between their late twen┬нti
body:
in Television |   	June 8th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

A great many, and per┬нhaps the major┬нi┬нty of Amer┬нi┬нcans now between their late twen┬нties and ear┬нly six┬нties, have spent time in Mis┬нter RogersтАЩ neigh┬нbor┬нhood. My own peri┬нod of reg┬нu┬нlar vis┬нi┬нta┬нtion would have been in the nine┬нteen-eight┬нies, a decade when Fred Rogers intro┬нduced his preschool-age view┬нers to guest stars from Lou Fer┬нrig┬нno, in and out of Incred┬нi┬нble Hulk make┬нup, to a ten-year-old boy with spina bifi┬нda. He also took on geopo┬нlit┬нi┬нcal issues, up to and includ┬нing mutu┬нal┬нly assured nuclear destruc┬нtion, and social ones, as on the mem┬нo┬нrable тАЬdivorce weekтАЭ of 1981. Such top┬нi┬нcal broad┬нcasts were mixed in with re-runs pro┬нduced as far back as 1969, the year Mis┬нter Rogers got the coun┬нtryтАЩs atten┬нtion by invit┬нing Offi┬нcer Clem┬нmons to share his wad┬нing pool.


	

What those of us then tun┬нing in did┬нnтАЩt see was any┬нthing from the first, black-and-white sea┬нson of Mis┬нter RogersтАЩ Neigh┬нbor┬нhood, which com┬нprised an aston┬нish┬нing┬а130 episodes that aired in 1968 alone. You can watch the series pre┬нmiere at the top of the post, just recent┬нly uploaded onto the showтАЩs new offi┬нcial chan┬нnel.
It may come as a shock to see a 39-year-old Mis┬нter Rogers, whom most of us remem┬нber as the embod┬нi┬нment of avun┬нcu┬нlar┬нi┬нty or even grand┬нfa┬нther┬нli┬нness. But whatтАЩs even more strik┬нing, if unsur┬нpris┬нing, is that his onscreen per┬нsona, with its dis┬нin┬нcli┬нna┬нtion to talk down to chil┬нdren, nev┬нer real┬нly changed. That sure┬нly owes to its appar┬нent iden┬нti┬нty with his off┬нscreen per┬нsona: as he liked to put it, тАЬkids can spot a pho┬нny a mile away.тАЭ


	

тАЬAside from clips and com┬нpi┬нla┬нtions,тАЭ writes the New York┬аTimesтАЩ Sopan Deb, тАЬthe chan┬нnel will make a selec┬нtion of full-length episodes avail┬нable glob┬нal┬нly for the first time as well as some that havenтАЩt aired in sev┬нer┬нal decades on PBS sta┬нtions.тАЭ With the showтАЩs 60th anniver┬нsary com┬нing up the year after next, the time does seem right to make as many of its 895 episodes as pos┬нsi┬нble avail┬нable to a new gen┬нer┬нa┬нtion. As of now, the chan┬нnel also offers the episodes with Offi┬нcer Clem┬нmons and the pool, Koko the Goril┬нla,┬аand the mes┬нmer┬нiz┬нing look inside the cray┬нon fac┬нto┬нry. ThereтАЩs even the crossover between Mis┬нter Rogers and Bill Nye the Sci┬нence Guy from 1997, by which time the lat┬нter had become a tele┬нvi┬нsion icon to us mil┬нlen┬нni┬нals.┬аThough we prob┬нa┬нbly did┬нnтАЩt catch his vis┬нit at the time, we can now┬аkeep it book┬нmarked to show our own kids тАФ assum┬нing they donтАЩt dis┬нcov┬нer it first.
Relat┬нed Con┬нtent:
Mr. Rogers Takes Break┬нdanc┬нing Lessons from a 12-Year-Old (1985)
Mr. RogersтАЩ Nine Rules for Speak┬нing to Chil┬нdren (1977)
Mis┬нter Rogers Cre┬нates a Prime Time TV Spe┬нcial to Help Par┬нents Talk to Their Chil┬нdren About the Assas┬нsi┬нna┬нtion of Robert F. Kennedy (1968)
Mr. Rogers Intro┬нduces Kids to Exper┬нi┬нmen┬нtal Elec┬нtron┬нic Music by Bruce Haack & Esther Nel┬нson (1968)
Mis┬нter Rogers Accepts a Life┬нtime Achieve┬нment Award, and Helps You Thank Every┬нone Who Has Made a Dif┬нfer┬нence in Your Life
Watch the First Episode of Sesame Street and 140 Oth┬нer Free Episodes
Based in Seoul,┬аCol┬нin┬аMarshall┬аwrites and broad┬нcasts on cities, lan┬нguage, and cul┬нture. HeтАЩs the author of the newslet┬нter┬аBooks on Cities┬аas well as the books┬аэХЬъ╡н ьЪФьХ╜ ъ╕ИьзА┬а(No Sum┬нma┬нriz┬нing Korea) and┬аKore┬нan Newtro.┬аFol┬нlow him on the social net┬нwork for┬нmer┬нly known as Twit┬нter at┬а@colinmarshall.

title: How Nick DrakeтАЩs тАЬRiver ManтАЭ Has Captivated Generation after Generation of Listeners
description: In 1999, Volk┬нswa┬нgen aired a tele┬нvi┬нsion com┬нmer┬нcial for the┬аGolf Mk3 Cabrio. Deal┬нer┬нships were soon inun┬нdat┬нed with calls, as pop┬нu┬нlar
body:
In 1999, Volk┬нswa┬нgen aired a tele┬нvi┬нsion com┬нmer┬нcial for the┬аGolf Mk3 Cabrio. Deal┬нer┬нships were soon inun┬нdat┬нed with calls, as pop┬нu┬нlar cul┬нture his┬нto┬нry remem┬нbers it, but not from peo┬нple inquir┬нing about the car. Rather, they were des┬нper┬нate to know the name of the song sound┬нtrack┬нing the adтАЩs footage of a top-down night dri┬нve to a house par┬нty. For all they knew, it was a new sin┬нgle from an up-and-com┬нing young man with an acoustic gui┬нtar and sen┬нsi┬нtiv┬нi┬нty exquis┬нite enough to cut through the sound and fury of turn-of-the-mil┬нlen┬нni┬нum pop. In fact, the song had come out 27 years before, and the artist had been dead for 25 of them. Thus began the obscure Eng┬нlish singer-song┬нwriter Nick DrakeтАЩs belat┬нed ascent to star┬нdom.
тАЬPink Moon,тАЭ the song from the VW Spot (a late replace┬нment for The ChurchтАЩs eight┬нies hit тАЬUnder the Milky WayтАЭ), was the title cut from DrakeтАЩs third and final album, which closed a record┬нing career not even three years long. It had begun in 1969, with the debut┬аFive Leaves Left. If lis┬нten┬нers of the late nineties curi┬нous enough to pick it up тАФ or, as had just become pos┬нsi┬нble, down┬нload it from file-shar┬нing net┬нworks тАФ could hard┬нly have been dis┬нap┬нpoint┬нed, they still would┬нnтАЩt have been pre┬нpared for its sec┬нond track, тАЬRiv┬нer Man.тАЭ
Described by Ian Mac┬нDon┬нald as тАЬone of the sky-high clas┬нsics of post-war Eng┬нlish pop┬нu┬нlar music,тАЭ the song com┬нbines DrakeтАЩs haunt┬нing┬нly evoca┬нtive lyri┬нcism and uncon┬нven┬нtion┬нal gui┬нtar tun┬нing with a rich lay┬нer of orches┬нtrat┬нed strings that stops just short of cloy┬нing, all in jazzy 5/4 time.


	

As music YouTu┬нber Charles Cor┬нnell points out in the video at the top of the post, youтАЩll no doubt rec┬нog┬нnize that time sig┬нna┬нture from Dave Brubeck┬нтАЩs тАЬTake Five,тАЭ which makes that high┬нly unusu┬нal rhythm feel nat┬нur┬нal. So does тАЬRiv┬нer Man,тАЭ though the more close┬нly you lis┬нten to it, the more musi┬нcal┬нly dar┬нing it sounds, even if you donтАЩt have the the┬нo┬нret┬нi┬нcal lan┬нguage to explain it as Cor┬нnell does. There is, for exam┬нple, no cho┬нrus, which could┬нnтАЩt have helped its chances of radio air┬нplay at the time, nor could the songтАЩs somber and reflec┬нtive mood. тАЬThe coun┬нter┬нcul┬нture was car┬нni┬нva┬нlesque, its opti┬нmism com┬нpul┬нso┬нry,тАЭ Mac┬нDon┬нald writes. тАЬDrake saw deep┬нer.тАЭ ItтАЩs hard┬нly implau┬нsi┬нble, in fact, to read the song as a Blakean and Bud┬нdhis┬нtic alle┬нgo┬нry of an indi┬нvid┬нual faced with a choice between the con┬нcrete, cycli┬нcal real┬нi┬нty of human affairs and the unknown realms beyond.


	

Drake com┬нposed тАЬRiv┬нer ManтАЭ dur┬нing his brief time at Cam┬нbridge, and the books writ┬нten about him quote acquain┬нtances from that peri┬нod describ┬нing it as a remark┬нable step for┬нward in his artis┬нtic evo┬нlu┬нtion. Dur┬нing the┬аFive Leaves Left ses┬нsions, he sang and played gui┬нtar live with the orches┬нtra, whose arrange┬нments (by the band┬нleader Har┬нry Robin┬нson, then known on British TV for his nov┬нel┬нty band Lord Rock┬нing┬нhamтАЩs XI) filled space Drake had delib┬нer┬нate┬нly left in the com┬нpo┬нsi┬нtion. The strings, in oth┬нer words, werenтАЩt an incon┬нgru┬нous attempt at sweet┬нen┬нing, as Phil Spec┬нtor would per┬нform on the Bea┬нt┬нlesтАЩ тАЬThe Long and Wind┬нing RoadтАЭ the fol┬нlow┬нing year, but an inte┬нgral part of the song. DrakeтАЩs solo per┬нfor┬нmance of it on BBC Radio 2тАЩs Night Ride (a broad┬нcast host┬нed by none oth┬нer than John Peel) sounds cap┬нti┬нvat┬нing, but incom┬нplete. On the Five Leaves Left ver┬нsion, every ele┬нment works togeth┬нer to make тАЬRiv┬нer ManтАЭ endur┬нing тАФ┬аand, in every sense, tran┬нscen┬нdent.
Relat┬нed con┬нtent:
A Doc┬нu┬нmen┬нtary Intro┬нduc┬нtion to Nick Drake, Whose Haunt┬нing & Influ┬нen┬нtial Songs Came Into the World 50 Years Ago Today
How John Lennon Wrote the Bea┬нt┬нlesтАЩ Best Song, тАЬA Day in the LifeтАЭ
How Joni Mitchell Wrote тАЬWood┬нstock,тАЭ the Song that Defined the Leg┬нendary Music Fes┬нti┬нval, Even Though She WasnтАЩt There (1969)
How Grace Slick Wrote тАЬWhite Rab┬нbitтАЭ: The 1960s Clas┬нsic Inspired by LSD, Lewis Car┬нroll, Miles DavisтАЩ Sketch┬нes of Spain, and Hyp┬нo┬нcrit┬нi┬нcal Par┬нents
Paul Simon Tells the Sto┬нry of How He Wrote тАЬBridge Over Trou┬нbled WaterтАЭ (1970)
How a Fake Car┬нtoon Band Made тАЬSug┬нar Sug┬нarтАЭ the Biggest Sell┬нing Hit Sin┬нgle of 1969
Based in Seoul,┬аCol┬нin┬аMarshall┬аwrites and broad┬нcasts on cities, lan┬нguage, and cul┬нture. HeтАЩs the author of the newslet┬нter┬аBooks on Cities┬аas well as the books┬аэХЬъ╡н ьЪФьХ╜ ъ╕ИьзА┬а(No Sum┬нma┬нriz┬нing Korea) and┬аKore┬нan Newtro.┬аFol┬нlow him on the social net┬нwork for┬нmer┬нly known as Twit┬нter at┬а@colinmarshall.

title: Dave Krugman's 'WINDOWS' Captures a Multitudinous Portrait of New York City
description: As Dave Krugman traverses the streets of New York City, camera in hand, he seeks patterns. Throughout his wide-ranging practice spanning portraits
body:
As Dave Krugman traverses the streets of New York City, camera in hand, he seeks patterns. Throughout his wide-ranging practice spanning portraits, automobiles, tourism, and more, he studies тАЬhumanityтАЩs intersection with cities and how people are influenced by their immediate environment,тАЭ says a statement. Whether in the countryside or the middle of an urban hub, rhythms and typologies emerge.
The Brooklyn-based photographer began documenting windows throughout New York City several years ago, and his aptly named series compiles a cross-section of his captures over the past half decade. Taken at night, these virtually infinite portals into individual lives are illuminated from within. Krugman focuses only on the light, shadow, and things placed just inside, such as cat beds, curtains, and holiday decorations that anyone can see as they pass by on the street. Each window on its own is a curiosity; arranged in a grid, they comprise a medley and a kind of portrait of the city.

тАЬNew York, despite being so densely populated, can often be a lonely place,тАЭ Krugman says. тАЬBy documenting the individuality and variation of these windows, and then sequencing them into randomized grids, weтАЩre able to recreate the sense of belonging and sonder that one feels while wandering the storied streets of this beautiful metropolis.тАЭ
Leaning into the concept of тАЬsonder,тАЭ which is an awareness of the immense complexity of othersтАЩ lives in addition to our own, the artist nudges our recognition of the unique experiences of the people behind these windows. тАЬTo me, WINDOWS is a reflection about how our similarities to each other overpower our differences, and how our isolation is ultimately overwhelmed by our sense of community and togetherness,тАЭ he says.
The search for patterns continues to inspire Krugman on wanderings throughout New York City. He likens the experience to foraging or collecting seashells on the coast. тАЬThat excitement and surprise of the variance and тАШsuccessтАЩ of finding a тАШgood oneтАЩ is rooted in our deepest evolutionary instincts,тАЭ he says. тАЬLayering that tendency over the built world, which often feels more foreign and unnatural, helps to remind me that we are still an extension of that natural worldтАФstill a part of itтАФeven if this particular forest is one of steel, stone, and glass.тАЭ
Krugman is currently organizing a project to be released in two phases, including a digital art sale with Transient Labs and a physical print sale. Updates will be released via his newsletter. Krugman is also the founder of┬аALLSHIPS, which he describes as тАЬa culture companyтАЭ for artists. See more on his Instagram.

schema.headline: Dave Krugman's 'WINDOWS' Captures a Multitudinous Portrait of New York City

title: Discover MA Arts and Cultural Enterprise at Central Saint Martins
description:
Announcement
                Develop business skills for cultural management and production on this flexible, part-time online M
body:
Announcement
                Develop business skills for cultural management and production on this flexible, part-time online Masters.┬а
            
                
                                
                            
                
            
                
        
    
        
            Thinking about a career in the cultural sector? MA Arts and Cultural Enterprise at Central Saint Martins equips students with critical and creative thinking to lead cultural projects globally.The course has been developed in response to the increasing need for multi-skilled individuals who can apply creativity to management, collaboration and cultural leadership. It is designed for emerging and established creative practitioners, cultural producers and professionals looking to expand their practice through critical, entrepreneurial and collaborative approaches.┬аWith cohorts across London and Hong Kong, MA Arts and Cultural Enterprise foregrounds peer and collaborative learning, and benefits from the cultural diversity and intercultural dialogue initiated throughout its teaching. Graduates leave the course with the skills and confidence to become active creators, producers, entrepreneurs and leaders within the cultural sector.┬аStudents are encouraged to apply course skills to their own professional backgrounds and interests. This blended-learning delivery mode is designed to┬а support those already in employment or with other commitments. Teaching is delivered both online and through intensive face-to-face weekends at Central Saint MartinsтАЩ KingтАЩs Cross campus (for the London-based cohort) and at HKU SPACEтАЩs Hong Kong campus (for the Hong Kong-based cohort).┬аJoin the upcoming Online Event for MA Arts and Cultural Enterprise to learn more about this unique course, hear directly from the course team, and explore whether this could be the right opportunity to shape your future.┬а┬а┬аRegister for the event on Tuesday, June 23, 2026 from 12pm-1pm (BST) (7-8:00am EDT)┬аTo learn more, visit arts.ac.uk.

title: Indian Theater: Native Performance, Art, and Self-Determination since 1969
description:
Announcement
                Featuring live presentations, this exhibition grounds performance as the foundation of contemporary Nati
body:
Announcement
                Featuring live presentations, this exhibition grounds performance as the foundation of contemporary Native Art. On view at SITE Santa Fe June 5тАУSeptember 7, 2026.
            
                
                                
                            
                
            
                
        
            Performance view from Spiderwoman Theater, "Lysistrata Numbah!" Performed in Denmark. Performers (L-R): Kachaka Snipe, Lisa Mayo, Sylvia Robinson, Muriel Miguel, Gloria Miguel (photo Martin S. Selway)
    
        
            Curated by Candice Hopkins (citizen of Carcross/Tagish First Nation), Indian Theater: Native Performance, Art, and Self-Determination since 1969 is the first major exhibition to position performance as an origin point for contemporary Native art. It traces a lineage of artistic experimentation that emerged in the late 1960s, a period defined by political activism, renewed engagement with Indigenous aesthetic traditions, and a profound reassertion of identity and self-determination.It is grounded in the pivotal year 1969, when Indian Theatre: An Artistic Experiment in Process was first published in Santa Fe at the Institute of American Indian Arts (IAIA) and the Indians of All Tribes occupied Alcatraz Island.┬аThe exhibition brings together over 100 artworks by over 40 artists and collectives. Across film, video, sculpture, painting, drawing, beadwork, and archival materials, the exhibition demonstrates the breadth of Native artistic practices while emphasizing performance as their generative core.Generously supported by: the Carl and Marilynn Thoma Foundation, Teiger Foundation, Nusenda Foundation, Century Bank, Diane and Werner Grob, Helen Kornblum, Courtney Finch Taylor and Scott Taylor, the SITE Santa Fe Board of Directors, and contributions made to the SITE Santa Fe Exhibitions Fund. Additional support is provided by the City of Santa Fe Arts and Culture Department and the 1% Lodgers Tax, and the County of Santa Fe LodgersтАЩ Tax.About SITE Santa FeGuided by artists, rooted in New Mexico, SITE Santa Fe celebrates contemporary creative expression. SITE Santa Fe is an internationally recognized destination for art and artists and a community hub for creativity and learning. Founded in 1995 to establish the first international contemporary art biennial in the United States, SITE Santa Fe is a non-collecting art institution committed to supporting new developments in contemporary art, encouraging artistic exploration, and expanding community through robust year-round programming.┬аTo learn more, visit sitesantafe.org.
                  
                      
                        Announcement
                      
                      
                        Exhibition Announcement
                      
                      
                        Sponsored
                      
                      
                        SITE Santa Fe
                      
                      
                        New Mexico

title: Listening to Our Queer & Trans Elders
description:
Daily Newsletter
                We kick off our Pride Month series with painter Jamie Nares. Plus, a sports betting company mak
body:
Daily Newsletter
                We kick off our Pride Month series with painter Jamie Nares. Plus, a sports betting company makes its first foray into the art market.
            
                
                                
                            
                
            
        
            Today, weтАЩre excited to kick off our annual Pride series with the first of several interviews with queer and trans elders in the art community. First up is Senior Editor Valentina Di LisciaтАЩs moving conversation with British painter Jamie Nares, who opens up about embracing her identity as a trans woman, finding belonging in New York City, and what sheтАЩs working on next. After all, she says, тАЬIтАЩm now 72 and IтАЩve never been so full of ideas in my life.тАЭMeanwhile, in the news, what do you get when you mix the art market with gambling? ThatтАЩs what Kalshi, a US government-regulated sports betting company, is about to find out. Matt Stromberg reports today on its new art offshoot, which it claims can help democratize the famously elitist market by allowing everyday people to buy in. LetтАЩs see how it goes тАФ place your bets now.тАФLakshmi Rivera Amin, associate editor


	
		
			Jamie NaresтАЩs Enduring Romance With the Brushstroke
		
	
	
		In sublime canvases animated by choreographies of sweeping motion, Jamie Nares captures the bravura of a brushstroke. The London-born artist has also made experimental films, photography, and music rooted in the spirit of the No Wave movement into which she was thrust when she relocated to New York City in the mid-1970s. 
      
      In our interview, Nares, who came out as transgender in 2019 and changed her name in 2024, leads us through her distinct personal and artistic evolutions, journeys paved by a search for truth. Insistent on finding тАЬthe essences of things,тАЭ on тАЬstripping away whatтАЩs superfluous,тАЭ the artist articulates a poetics of life and identity.
	
	
		Read More
	


                    
                    
                    
                        
                            Divination, Mark Making, Boxing, & Drawing: тАЬTracey RoseтАЭ at Ruby CityOpening June 6 in San Antonio, Texas, тАЬTracey RoseтАЭ offers viewers an intimate look at the artistтАЩs groundbreaking multidisciplinary practice including performance and drawings.
                        
                        
                            Learn more
                        
                    
            NewsAmid heightened scrutiny over insider trading and state gambling regulations, the prediction market platform Kalshi has ventured into the rarefied space where fine art and big money meet, launching a┬аsection dedicated to art markets.The New School in Manhattan has laid off 19 full-time faculty and 68 staff members as it confronts a┬а$160 million budget deficit┬аattributed to the COVID-19 pandemic and declining student enrollment.┬аFrom Our Critics

                    
                    
                    
                        With hands-on, interdisciplinary coursework; high-level networking opportunities; and an engaging internship, the three-semester MA in Art Market Studies at FIT prepares students to shepherd artists in their careers, buy and sell artworks, and succeed at the business of art.
                        
                            Learn more
                        
                    
            Opinions

From the Archive
                  
                      
                        Daily Newsletter
                      
                      
                        Newsletter

title: SothebyтАЩs To Offer $60 M. Modigliani From Exhibition Once Shuttered For Indeceny, and More: Morning Links for June 8, 2026
description:
To receive┬аMorning Links┬аin your inbox every weekday,┬аsign┬аup┬аfor our┬аBreakfast with ARTnews┬аnewsletter.

	Good Morning!


SothebyтАЩs attempt at a pr
body:
To receive┬аMorning Links┬аin your inbox every weekday,┬аsign┬аup┬аfor our┬аBreakfast with ARTnews┬аnewsletter.

	Good Morning!


SothebyтАЩs attempt at a private sale of Pace founder Arne GlimcherтАЩs Jackson Pollock painting was called off for lack of bidders.
The chairman of the US Commission of Fine Arts participated in the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum, the first US official to do so in nearly a decade.
SothebyтАЩs will offer Amedeo ModiglianiтАЩs┬аNu assis au collier┬а(Seated Nude with Necklace), 1917, later this month.


	
		The Headlines	

	BEST KEPT SECRET. According to multiple sources speaking to┬аARTnews, SothebyтАЩs recent, and apparently┬аfirst serious attempt┬аat a private, or тАЬsecret,тАЭ auction couldnтАЩt get off the ground. With an unusual degree of secrecy, the New York auction house organized a sale early last week of Pace founder┬аArne GlimcherтАШs monumental┬аJackson Pollock┬аpainting,┬аNumber 19, 1951, measuring nearly five feet tall and four feet wide, estimated at $50 million. But as┬аARTnewsтАШs┬аDaniel Cassady┬аwrites, тАЬif the plan was to make sure nobody noticed, it worked remarkably well.тАЭ The auction was ultimately called off after SothebyтАЩs couldnтАЩt find enough bidders. Since the Covid-19 pandemic,┬аChristieтАЩs┬аhas developed the private, or secret, auction format for top works that owners donтАЩt want to expose to the scrutiny of traditional evening sales.

	
		Related Articles
	


	тАЬGOOD HELLOтАЭ TO RUSSIA.┬аOn Friday,┬аRodney Mims Cook Jr., the chairman of the US Commission of Fine Arts, spoke at a major economic forum hosted by Russian President┬аVladimir Putin┬аin St. Petersburg, becoming the first US official to attend the event in nearly a decade, according to reports. Dubbed the тАЬRussian Davos,тАЭ the┬аSt. Petersburg International Economic Forum┬а(SPIEF) is designed to attract foreign investment but has seen a drop in attendance from high-profile Western leaders since RussiaтАЩs 2022 invasion of Ukraine. Cook led a US delegation to the forum and spoke during FridayтАЩs plenary session. тАЬYou have a beautiful hometown,тАЭ Cook told Putin, according to┬аReuters. тАЬI do give a good hello from your friend, President Trump.тАЭ On Thursday, Cook also attended a roundtable titled тАЬRussia-USA: Dialogue of Cultures,тАЭ moderated by Russian cultural envoy┬аMikhail Shvydkoy, who was also key in orchestrating RussiaтАЩs participation in this yearтАЩs Venice Biennale, the┬аArt Newspaper┬аreports. Among the speakers was also US actor┬аSteven Seagal, whom the Russian Foreign Ministry appointed as a special envoy for Russian-American тАЬhumanitarian relationsтАЭ in 2018.
	
	
		The Digest	

	At this monthтАЩs London auctions, SothebyтАЩs will offer┬аAmedeo ModiglianiтАШs┬аNu assis au collier┬а(Seated Nude with Necklace), 1917, a rare piece from the artistтАЩs December 1917 exhibition that police shuttered for indecency, estimated at $60.6 million.┬а[Artnet News]
	

	Austrian photographer┬аElfie Semotan┬аhas died at age 84.┬а[Monopol]

	DIGITAL DIVIDE. Dataland, the worldтАЩs first museum dedicated to AI-generated art, founded by┬аRefik Anadol┬аand his wife, the painter┬аEfsun Erkilic, is preparing to open in Los Angeles later this month. As the┬аLos Angeles Times┬аreports, it will feature an тАЬimmersive rainforest journeyтАЭ powered by an AI system called the Large Nature Model, which uses data gathered тАЬethicallyтАЭ from the environment. тАЬThe system is the art,тАЭ Anadol told the┬аLos Angeles Times┬аon a recent preview of the exhibition тАЬMachine Dreams: Rainforest,тАЭ which includes options for selecting what the show will smell like, and the possibility of sharing visitor biometrics. But as the┬аNew York Times┬аnotes in its┬аpreview, AnadolтАЩs creations тАФ exemplified by the popular 2022тАУ23 installation┬аUnsupervised, at New YorkтАЩs MoMA тАФ also embody the art worldтАЩs тАЬdigital divide.тАЭ Is┬аUnsupervised┬атАЬa massive techno lava lamp,тАЭ as┬аJerry Saltz┬аcalled it, or тАЬan early masterpiece of AI-generated art,тАЭ per critic┬аSebastian Smee? Still, museums are increasingly embracing AnadolтАЩs vision. тАЬThereтАЩs not going to be a future in which this kind of work is not happening,тАЭ┬аMichael Govan, CEO of the Los Angeles County Museum of Art (LACMA), told the┬аNew York Times. тАЬItтАЩs like Marcel Duchamp тАФ if you know whatтАЩs behind it, youтАЩre open to understanding it.
